scope: New guide topic recommendations to build topical authority around home sauna and drive organic traffic
keySignal: Top GSC performer is 'when-not-to-sauna' at position 9.2 with 493 impressions — the safety/contra-indication cluster is the site's strongest authority signal. Build around it.

executiveSummary.siteStrength: The 'when not to sauna' page is the clearest evidence of where the site has early authority: safety, contra-indications, and 'who should NOT do this' queries. This aligns with E-E-A-T because health sites and AI search engines prioritize trustworthy, cautionary content over promotional content.
executiveSummary.coreOpportunity: The site has 24 guides but is missing two full content clusters that home sauna buyers search heavily during the 90-180 day research phase: (1) the beginner/first-use cluster, and (2) the practical ownership cluster (maintenance, sizing, permit, space planning). Both are low-competition, high-commercial-intent, and link naturally to existing guides.
executiveSummary.keyGap: No competitor owns the beginner funnel from 'I am curious about saunas' through to 'I am ready to buy a home sauna.' The guides that currently rank for beginner queries are all on vendor blogs (havenofheat.com, mysaunaworld.com, finnmarksauna.com) — not neutral editorial brands. A trusted editorial voice with strong internal linking can outrank them.
executiveSummary.aiSearchNote: Perplexity and ChatGPT pull heavily from content with direct, quotable answer paragraphs, specific data points (percentages, durations, temperatures), and FAQ schema. All 10 recommended guides below should be structured with an opening direct-answer paragraph, a comparison table or numbered list, and FAQ schema.

topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: All ranking pages are on vendor/retailer blogs. None are from a neutral editorial brand. They all funnel toward their own products. A genuinely neutral, atmospheric guide from sauna.guide has a trust advantage.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: The first-time sauna experience, written with atmosphere. What to expect, what to do, how to feel — not a product pitch. Title direction: 'Your First Sauna: What to Expect and How to Make It Count.' Lead with sensation and ritual, not a bullet list of rules. This is the guide that converts curious readers into newsletter subscribers.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational — person is exploring, not yet a buyer. High newsletter signup potential.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Newsletter CTA (primary). Soft link to buying guide for readers who want to own one.
topKeywordOpportunities.seasonality: Evergreen — small January peak (New Year wellness resolutions)
topKeywordOpportunities.featuredSnippetTarget: What should a first-time sauna user expect? Answer paragraph in opening.

topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: The SERPs are dominated by mattress and sleep-device brands. They cover sauna superficially as a sleep aid rather than as a practice. None have deep sauna expertise. A dedicated sauna guide site with cited science will have authority advantage.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: Science-backed but atmospheric. Open with the mechanism (post-sauna core temperature drop triggers melatonin — 36% shorter sleep onset latency in studies). Then build into a practical evening sauna ritual. Include optimal timing (1-2 hours before bed), duration, and the cool-down protocol. This aligns directly with brand voice — 'five minutes of stillness' as a sleep practice.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational — health-motivated, high newsletter signup potential. Person is not yet a buyer but is bonding with the practice.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Newsletter CTA. Affiliate opportunity: sleep tracking devices (Oura, Whoop) used to measure sauna sleep impact.
topKeywordOpportunities.featuredSnippetTarget: How long before bed should you use a sauna? Answer: 1-2 hours.
topKeywordOpportunities.keyStatToLead: 83% of regular sauna users report improved sleep. One study found evening sauna sessions increase deep sleep within the first 2 hours by up to 70%.

topKeywordOpportunities.keyword: sauna frequency — how often should you use a sauna
topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: Most articles answer this in 200 words or with a generic '2-3 times per week.' The Huberman Lab newsletter is the strongest piece but it lives behind a newsletter wall and is not a standalone page. There is no clear featured-snippet-optimized guide from a neutral sauna brand.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: Lead with the Kuopio study data (2,315 Finnish men, 20 years): 4-7 sessions per week = 40% reduction in cardiovascular mortality vs once per week. Then build a practical frequency guide by goal — cardiovascular health (4-7x/week at 80-100°C), recovery (2-3x/week post-workout), sleep (3-5x/week, 1-2 hours before bed), stress relief (as needed). This is the guide that turns a curious reader into a committed practitioner — and a newsletter subscriber.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational with practice-building intent. High newsletter conversion potential.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Newsletter CTA. Natural bridge to home sauna content ('if you want to sauna 4-7x per week, a home sauna changes the math').
topKeywordOpportunities.featuredSnippetTarget: How often should you use a sauna? Target the PAA box with a direct answer: 'For cardiovascular health benefits, 4-7 sessions per week of 15-20 minutes at 80-100°C is the evidence-supported target. For general wellness, 2-3 sessions per week is a well-researched starting point.'

topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: Every ranking page is a vendor site. Content is accurate but promotional. None have a strong editorial brand or atmospheric voice. This is a pure authority gap for a neutral guide site.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: The definitive reference guide for all sauna types. Structure: traditional/Finnish sauna temps (80-100°C / 176-212°F), infrared sauna temps (43-60°C / 110-140°F), steam room temps (40-50°C / 104-122°F). Then: beginner progressions (start at 70°C, build over weeks), the relationship between temperature and duration, and how to use bench height to control perceived heat. Include a simple reference table. This page earns links as a reference — it is the kind of structured, factual, well-organized content that AI search cites.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational — beginner and intermediate users. Also a purchase-stage query ('what temperature does an infrared sauna reach?' is asked by buyers comparing specs).
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Affiliate links to heaters. Internal bridge to /guides/sauna-heater-sizing-guide.
topKeywordOpportunities.schemaOpportunity: FAQ schema: 'What temperature should a sauna be?', 'What temperature is too hot for a sauna?', 'What temperature does an infrared sauna reach?'

topKeywordOpportunities.keyword: sauna mental health benefits — depression, anxiety, stress
topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: No neutral editorial brand owns this cluster. Most articles are from treatment centers or sauna vendors with a conflict of interest. Healthline and MedicalNewsToday have not written dedicated mental health + sauna pieces — this is a genuine gap for established health publishers, let alone sauna-specific brands.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: The science is strong and underreported: heat exposure activates the same serotonin pathways as antidepressants, 29% drop in resting cortisol after regular use, 78% reduction in psychotic episode risk in cohort studies, strong evidence for anxiety reduction via parasympathetic activation. Lead with the science, then shift to the practice: the sauna as a designated 20-minute window where you are not allowed to do anything but exist. This aligns perfectly with brand voice ('five minutes of stillness') and is a powerful newsletter signup hook.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational — mental health and wellness audience. High emotional resonance, high newsletter conversion.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Newsletter CTA (primary). This audience is wellness-oriented but not necessarily a home sauna buyer — keep monetization soft.
topKeywordOpportunities.keyStatToLead: A 2016 study found whole-body hyperthermia reduced Hamilton Depression Rating Scale scores by 50% compared to placebo — one session, sustained for 6 weeks. β-endorphin release from sauna use is measurably similar to the effect of intense exercise.

topKeywordOpportunities.keyword: home sauna size guide — how big does a home sauna need to be
competitorsSERPNow: Multiple vendor blogs, no dominant content-first brand
topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: This is a research-phase buyer query with weak SERP competition. Most results are vendor pages with minimal editorial investment. No dedicated guide exists from a content-first brand.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: Practical decision guide structured by use case: 1-person (3x4 ft minimum), 2-person (4x6 ft), family (6x8 ft+). Include ceiling height (7 ft minimum), bench depth (600mm per person for sitting, 2000mm to lie down), door clearance, and ventilation requirements. Add a simple selection table. This page naturally earns a position in the 'I am about to buy' research phase and bridges to the buying guide, cost guide, and electrical guide.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Pre-purchase research — serious buyer. High affiliate and internal linking value.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Affiliate links to recommended sauna kits by size bracket. Internal bridge to cost guide.
topKeywordOpportunities.schemaOpportunity: HowTo schema for the size calculation process. FAQ: 'How big does a home sauna need to be?', 'What is the minimum size for a 2-person sauna?'

topKeywordOpportunities.keyword: sauna after alcohol — is it safe
topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: Every ranking page is a vendor with a sauna to sell. They are incentivized to be cautionary but also reassuring ('just wait a few hours'). A neutral guide that cites the actual PubMed research (alcohol was a factor in half of Finnish sauna fatalities) is more trustworthy and will rank above vendor content for health-query searchers. This is also a PAA box opportunity for the 'when not to sauna' cluster — it feeds authority back to that page.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: Honest, direct, safety-first. The Finnish data is stark. Lead with the real risk, give the clear answer (avoid saunas within 12-24 hours of significant drinking), and explain the physiology (alcohol + heat causes dramatic BP drop, impaired thermoregulation, and dehydration compounding effect). This is an extension of the 'when not to sauna' cluster that is already the site's strongest performer. Strong internal link bridge to that page.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational — safety query. Person is asking because they have had drinks or are planning to. High urgency answer format.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: No direct affiliate opportunity. High E-E-A-T signal. Newsletter CTA at end — 'the practice, done right.'
topKeywordOpportunities.featuredSnippetTarget: Is it safe to use a sauna after drinking alcohol? Aim for the PAA box: 'No. Alcohol reduces blood pressure significantly during sauna use and was a factor in approximately half of sauna-related fatalities in Finland. Wait at least 12-24 hours after significant alcohol consumption before using a sauna.'

topKeywordOpportunities.keyword: home sauna maintenance — how to clean a sauna
topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: Entirely dominated by vendor sites with SEO-thin content. Maintenance guides rank because the query has very little competition — a well-structured, neutral guide would rank quickly. This is also a post-purchase query cluster that home sauna owners actively search. It builds authority with existing owners as an audience, not just buyers.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: The owner's guide to a long-lasting sauna. Structured as weekly, monthly, and annual tasks. Weekly: bench wipe-down with baking soda solution, air out for 30 minutes after each use. Monthly: deep clean with appropriate wood cleaner, inspect door seals. Annual: re-stack and inspect stones, sand benches if needed, check electrical connections. Include a simple checklist graphic. This page wins trust with sauna owners — a segment with high newsletter retention and word-of-mouth value.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational — existing sauna owner. Lower buyer intent but high retention/loyalty value.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Affiliate links to sauna cleaning products, wood oil/treatment products. Low but consistent.
topKeywordOpportunities.schemaOpportunity: HowTo schema with step-by-step maintenance process.

topKeywordOpportunities.keyword: DIY sauna vs sauna kit vs pre-built sauna
competitorsSERPNow: fieldmag.com (one of the better neutral pieces)
topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: Most 'DIY vs kit' content comes from brands that sell kits. Field Mag has a neutral piece but it focuses on the author's personal DIY build rather than a decision framework. No content-first brand has built a true comparison guide for this decision that is both opinionated and structured.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: The buyer's honest decision framework. Three paths: DIY custom build ($4K-$12K materials + your time, requires carpentry skills and electrical permit knowledge), sauna kit ($3K-$15K, minimal tools needed, still requires electrical work), pre-built unit ($2K-$30K, plug-and-play, highest cost per feature). Structure around: cost, quality control, timeline, skill requirements, and long-term satisfaction. Include the real total costs the Reddit community always reports — delivery shocks, electrician costs, foundation prep. Close with the decision matrix: who should choose each path.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Pre-purchase decision — buyer is serious and comparing routes. High affiliate value.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Affiliate links to recommended kits and pre-built units. Strong commercial intent.

topKeywordOpportunities.keyword: sauna and cold plunge routine — how to combine them
topKeywordOpportunities.competitorWeakness: The site already has a contrast therapy guide (/guides/contrast-therapy-complete-guide). This slug targets the more common user query language ('sauna and cold plunge routine') that people actually type, versus the academic 'contrast therapy' framing. The existing guide ranks at position 13.8 — this new page targets the same audience with higher-volume keywords and then internally links to the existing contrast therapy guide, strengthening both.
topKeywordOpportunities.contentAngle: The practical routine guide, written as a ritual not a protocol. Open with the Nordic tradition (alternating heat and cold has been practiced for centuries — this is not a biohacking trend). Then give the actual routine: 10-15 min sauna at 80-90°C, 1-3 min cold plunge at 10-15°C, 5-10 min rest, repeat 2-3 cycles, always finish cold. Include timing, temperature settings, breath work during the cold plunge, and the mental shift that makes the cold plunge tolerable. This topic is trending hard (dominant on social since 2024) and has strong affiliate potential.
topKeywordOpportunities.userIntent: Informational with practice intent. High newsletter conversion — this audience actively seeks a practice they can commit to.
topKeywordOpportunities.monetization: Affiliate links to cold plunge tubs, ice makers. This is the highest affiliate-value topic on the list — cold plunge units range from $300 (inflatable) to $5,000+.
topKeywordOpportunities.seasonality: Peaks January-March (New Year resolution cycle). Steady year-round.
topKeywordOpportunities.note: This page and the existing contrast-therapy-complete-guide should be differentiated: contrast-therapy is the science and health evidence, this slug is the practical routine. They target different queries and different moments in the user journey.

quickWins.action: Write /guides/sauna-for-better-sleep. Vendor-dominated SERP, no editorial brand, strong science to cite. Low competition, strong brand voice fit.
Write /guides/sauna-temperature-guide as a structured reference table with beginner progressions by sauna type. Vendor-only SERP. Earns links as a reference.

quickWins.keyword: how often to use a sauna
quickWins.action: Write /guides/how-often-to-use-sauna. Lead with the Kuopio study data. Build into a goal-specific frequency guide. Natural PAA target.
Write /guides/sauna-after-drinking-alcohol. Short, direct, safety-first. Extends the 'when not to sauna' cluster which is already the site's strongest performer. Immediate PAA box opportunity.
Write /guides/home-sauna-maintenance-guide. Entirely vendor-dominated SERP. Low competition, quick win, builds loyalty with existing owners.

competitorGaps.topic: Trusted editorial voice for the beginner cluster
competitorGaps.opportunity: Every beginner sauna guide ranks on a vendor blog. A neutral, atmospheric editorial guide from sauna.guide can outrank them on trust signals. The guide should lead with experience and ritual, not product features.

competitorGaps.topic: Mental health as a primary sauna benefit
competitorGaps.opportunity: Healthline and MedicalNewsToday do not have dedicated sauna + mental health pieces. The science is strong (serotonin pathways, cortisol reduction, depression trial data). A neutral guide from a sauna-specific brand fills this gap and earns citations from both health searchers and AI search engines.

competitorGaps.topic: DIY vs kit vs pre-built comparison
competitorGaps.opportunity: The only neutral piece is a Field Mag personal narrative. No content-first brand has built an opinionated decision framework. The Reddit community (r/sauna, r/saunabuilding) constantly asks this — the guide that answers it definitively earns Reddit links.

competitorGaps.topic: Sauna maintenance and care for owners
competitorGaps.opportunity: Every maintenance guide is on a vendor site. The post-purchase owner audience is underserved by neutral content. Owning this cluster builds loyalty, reduces churn from the newsletter, and earns links from owner communities.

competitorGaps.topic: Specific PAA box capture around the 'when not to sauna' cluster
competitorGaps.opportunity: The site is already near page 1 for 'when not to sauna.' Satellite pages targeting related safety queries (alcohol, blood pressure, pregnancy, medications) will build cluster authority and pull that ranking up to positions 1-5. Each satellite page also targets its own PAA box.

cluster1_beginner.name: Beginner / First-Use Cluster
cluster1_beginner.rationale: No beginner pillar exists. The spokes exist but have no pillar to anchor them. Creating the beginner guide and linking it to all existing foundational guides significantly increases the authority signal for the entire beginner cluster.

cluster2_practice.name: Sauna Practice / Ritual Cluster
cluster2_practice.rationale: The science guide exists but has no frequency guide, sleep guide, or mental health guide pointing at it. These three new spokes build a full practice cluster that converts curious readers into committed practitioners and newsletter subscribers.

cluster3_safety.name: Safety / Contraindication Cluster (site's strongest signal)
spokes: /guides/sauna-health-risks — address blood pressure, pregnancy, medications (EXISTING or NEW)
cluster3_safety.rationale: The 'when not to sauna' page is the site's best performer. Building satellite pages around specific contraindications strengthens the cluster's authority and pulls all pages up. This is the highest-leverage cluster investment given the existing ranking signal.

cluster4_homeBuyer.name: Home Buyer Research Cluster
cluster4_homeBuyer.rationale: The buying guide exists but is missing the size guide, the DIY vs kit comparison, and the maintenance guide — all three are queries buyers research during the 90-180 day purchase cycle. Adding these spokes closes the pre-purchase research loop and positions the buying guide as the topical hub.

recommendations: Every guide must open with a direct-answer paragraph of 2-4 sentences. AI engines extract opening paragraphs for citations. Do not lead with context — lead with the answer.

Include specific cited data points in every guide: percentages, study populations, durations, temperatures. AI prefers specifics over generalities. Example: '4-7 sauna sessions per week reduces cardiovascular mortality by 40% (Laukkanen et al., 20-year cohort, n=2,315)' not 'frequent sauna use has cardiovascular benefits.'
Add FAQ schema to every new guide. Perplexity and ChatGPT pull FAQ schema directly. Target the PAA questions for each topic.
Publish sauna content in r/sauna and r/HomeImprovement with links to guides. Perplexity cites Reddit at 6.6% of responses — a linked Reddit thread increases citation probability.
Structure content with comparison tables where applicable. Tables are extracted and cited by AI search engines.

For the safety cluster specifically: AI engines prioritize health-cautionary content from authoritative sources. The 'when not to sauna' and 'sauna after alcohol' guides will be cited if they are clearly structured with a safety-first answer at the top.

issues: The contrast-therapy guide ranks at 13.8 despite being a strong piece — likely lacks internal links from newer guides. Cross-linking all new guides to it should lift position.
Safety guides (sauna-safety, when-not-to-sauna) are not interlinked with each other or with the beginner guide — each lives as an isolated page.

The existing 'when-not-to-sauna' page sits at 9.2 with 493 impressions and 0 clicks — title tag and meta description may not be click-compelling enough. Should be audited for CTR optimization before writing new content.
fixes: Audit and optimize the title tag and meta description of the 'when-not-to-sauna' page for CTR. At 493 impressions and 0 clicks at position 9.2, a CTR improvement alone could deliver 20-40 clicks/month without writing new content.
Add FAQ schema to all existing guides — this is a one-time SEO lift that requires no new content.
Create internal link hubs: add a 'Related Guides' section to the bottom of each existing page that links to thematically related guides. This builds cluster authority passively.
Ensure all new guides have JSON-LD schema: Article for editorial guides, HowTo for step-by-step guides, FAQ for all pages.

week1_2.action: Audit and optimize 'when-not-to-sauna' title tag and meta description for CTR
week1_2.rationale: 493 impressions at position 9.2 = 40-50 potential clicks/month at a 8-10% CTR. Currently at 0. This is the fastest traffic gain available on the entire site.
Lowest competition quick win. Extends the site's strongest cluster.
Low competition, high volume, strong brand voice fit, fast to write with available science.

week3_4.rationale: High volume, PAA box target, Huberman effect (his newsletter covers this but is not an open-web page that sauna.guide has to beat).
Reference-format page that earns links. Low competition, builds beginner cluster.

week5_6.rationale: Pillar for the beginner cluster. Requires more care to get the voice right — atmospheric, not a vendor tutorial.
Low competition, high buyer intent, closes a gap in the home buyer cluster.

week7_8.rationale: High authority signal for E-E-A-T. No editorial brand owns this cluster.
Vendor-only SERP. Fast to rank. Builds loyalty with owners.

week9_10.rationale: Most research-intensive piece. High buyer intent, strong affiliate value.
Targets high-volume query with better keyword alignment than existing contrast-therapy guide.
